info.title: Appen Platform API
info.description:
# Introduction
Hello, and welcome to Appen, the leading data annotation platform for Machine Learning. Our API enables developers to build applications that can programmatically create, edit, and launch Appen jobs, download results, and more! 

Appen uses a RESTful API that accepts data requests as URL-encoded key-value pairs. Responses are returned in JSON and authentication is key-based.

Before integrating with the API, we highly recommend you first build and run a job using our [graphical user interface](https://client.appen.com/sessions/new). Once you have run a job successfully and are satisfied with the results, you can automate the process using the Appen API.

# Authentication
You will need to supply your Appen API key to access the API. To find your API key:
1. Click the circular account icon located in the top right corner of any page and then select the Profile option from the dropdown.
![Appen Authentication](/assets/img/authentication1.png "Appen Authentication")
2. Click the API tab and you can use Generate New to create an API key. Note that creating a new API key will invalidate any previous API keys for this user so remember to update any code using the previous key.
![Appen API](/assets/img/authentication2.png "Appen API key")

For requests listed below, the API Key will be listed as `{api_key}`. 

The API key needs to be included in the header for every request: 
```
  headers: { 

   "AUTHORIZATION" => "Token token={api_key}" 

  } 
```

You may also see other variables, such as `{job_id}` for operations pertaining to a specific job, or `{team_id}` when completing operations relevant to an entire team (like listing all available jobs). Job ID's can be determined from the job listing page or the URL. A team id is visible in the URL of the Teams tab in the Account page. 
# Resources
There are three primary data objects on Appen that correspond to resources on the API:
## Jobs
A **Job** is an interface that connects your data to an online workforce.
  Each job on the Appen platform has data, instructions, customizable questions for your use case
  (written in **CML** [](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/202817989-CML-Custom-Markup-Language-Overview)),
  [test questions](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/202702985-How-to-Create-Test-Questions),
  and is worked on by *Contributors*. Contributors submit *Judgments* on the rows of data via a worker interface. 
    * All jobs in a single account can be found [on your jobs page](https://client.appen.com/jobs) and will be
    identified by a unique numeric id.
## Units (also known as 'Rows')
A **Unit** of data is uploaded to the job from your source data file via API or GUI. 
## Judgments 
A **Judgment** is the set of responses submitted by a contributor on a particular unit of data. It is
  recommended to collect multiple judgments and compare them to one another or aggregate to the top response. For
  each job, you can specify the number of judgments you would like each row to receive.
# Responses and Messaging
Each request to the Appen API returns an HTTP status code response and one or both of the following:
* An application response message (in JSON) 
* A JSON representation of the object (job, unit, or judgments) requested
See the below sections of different API Requests, Responses and Messaging for more information:
1. [Job Create/Update](/#tag/Job-CreateUpdate)
2. [Manage Job Data](/#tag/Manage-Job-Data)
3. [Job Status](/#tag/Job-Status)
4. [Monitor Contributors](/#tag/Monitor-Contributors)
5. [Manage Job Settings](/#tag/Manage-Job-Settings)
6. [Job Results](/#tag/Job-Results)
7. [Account Info](/#tag/Account-Info)
8. [Project Flow](/#tag/Project-Flow)

servers.description: APIs in Beta e.g. Project Flow

post.description:
**With no request body** - Creates a new Job with a Job ID—but without data, title, instructions, or other settings values.

**With optional params** - Creates a new Job with Job ID, Title, CML, and Instructions.

**See also:** [`POST /jobs/upload.json`](/#tag/Job-CreateUpdate/paths/~1jobs~1upload.json/post)

200.description: OK - Job Created

x-code-samples.label: cURL
x-code-samples.source:
curl -X POST \ "https://api.appen.com/v1/jobs.json" \ -H "Authorization: Token token={api_key}" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"job": { "title": "Select the animal types in the image.", "instructions": "

Some valid

html instructions

", "cml": "

Collect Information

\n
\n\n\n\n" } }'

get.summary: Paginated list all Jobs available to Account (by user or team id).
get.description:
Queries for all Jobs under the Account related to the API key in the
request.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Can be unordered, running, canceled, finished, or paused

parameters.name: created_at_greater_equal
parameters.description: Accepts dates in any of the following formats: 2020/03/15, 2020-03-15, 15-03-2020, 03/15/2020

parameters.name: created_at_less_equal
parameters.description: Accepts dates in any of the following formats: 2020/03/15, 2020-03-15, 15-03-2020, 03/15/2020

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Specify the name of a tag. Accepts multiple tags, separate tags with  commas i.e. tag1, tag2, tag3, ..., tagn

200.description:
JSON representation of the latest 10 Jobs. Use page={n} query to get
additional Jobs.

post.summary: Create a new job by uploading unit data.
post.description:
Creates a new Job and uploads rows comprised of the JSON or CSV data in
the request. Note that this request invokes the upload operation.

requestBody.description:
A **CSV** or **JSON Lines** formatted file of unit data to upload to
the Job once it is created.

200.description: OK - Job Created, Units uploaded

put.description:
Update various elements of a job. Your job must have a valid title,
instructions, and one "required" CML form element to be launched
successfully.

parameters.name: job[title]
parameters.description: Updates the title of the Job.

parameters.name: job[instructions]
parameters.description: Updates the instructions of the Job.

parameters.name: job[cml]
parameters.description: Updates the CML of the Job.

parameters.name: job[css]
parameters.description: Updates the CSS of the Job.

parameters.name: job[payment_cents]
parameters.description:
Determines the amount, in cents, that Contributors will be paid per
Task

parameters.name: job[judgments_per_unit]
parameters.description: Unit in the refers to a single row in the job

parameters.name: job[quiz_length_configured]
parameters.description: True, for custom number of test questions in Quiz mode

parameters.name: job[quiz_length]
parameters.description: Number of test questions in quiz

parameters.name: job[options][req_ttl_in_seconds]
parameters.description: Set the amount of time per assignment. Default is set to 30 minutes.

parameters.name: job[units_per_assignment]
parameters.description: Sets the number of Rows that will comprise one Page within a Job.

parameters.name: job[pages_per_assignment]
parameters.description:
Number of pages to split a single assignment into. 

For example, if Rows per Page is 20 and pages_per_assignment (aka page break up in the UI) is 2, then each page of work will contain 10 rows.

parameters.name: job[test_question_frequency]
parameters.description:
This value represents the number of test questions per X number of pages in Work Mode. You can configure the frequency to be X test questions per Y pages. 

In Work Mode, the default test question frequency is 1 per page. Please see the [Guide To: Test Question Settings (Quality Control)](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/202702975-Guide-To-Test-Question-Settings-Quality-Control-) article in the Success Center for more details.

parameters.name: job[test_question_frequency_page]
parameters.description:
This value represents the number of pages that X number of test questions will be seen in Work Mode. You can configure the frequency to be X test questions per Y pages.

In Work Mode, the default test question frequency is 1 per page. Please see the [Guide To: Test Question Settings (Quality Control)](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/202702975-Guide-To-Test-Question-Settings-Quality-Control-) article in the Success Center for more details.

parameters.name: job[variable_judgments_mode]
parameters.description:
Accepts "none", "auto_confidence" or "auto_match". Use in combination with
job[options][dynamic_judgment_fields], job[max_judgments_per_unit], and
job[min_unit_confidence] / job[options][auto_match_count] to set dynamic judgments. More info
[Job Launch > Job Settings > Guide to Dynamic Judgments](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/203219205-Job-Settings-Guide-to-Dynamic-Judgments)

parameters.name: job[options][dynamic_judgment_fields]
parameters.description:
CML form element name that determines row finalization for dynamic
judgments. More info in the Success center at
[Job Launch > Job Settings > Guide to Dynamic Judgments](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/203219205-Job-Settings-Guide-to-Dynamic-Judgments)

parameters.name: job[max_judgments_per_unit]
parameters.description:
Maximum number of judgments collected per row. More info in the Success center at
[Job Launch > Job Settings > Guide to Dynamic Judgments](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/203219205-Job-Settings-Guide-to-Dynamic-Judgments)

parameters.name: job[min_unit_confidence]
parameters.description:
Minimum confidence needed to finalize a row (a number from 0 to 1).
More info in the Success center at
[Job Launch > Job Settings > Guide to: Dynamic Judgments](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/203219205-Job-Settings-Guide-to-Dynamic-Judgments)

parameters.name: job[auto_order]
parameters.description: Automatically launch rows as they are added to the Job

parameters.name: job[auto_order_timout]
parameters.description:
The time in seconds before automatic ordering of any new rows will
occur. auto_order must be enabled.

parameters.name: job[auto_order_threshold]
parameters.description:
The job must have at least this many new rows—or the timeout must
have passed—before new rows will be ordered automatically. This
setting must be greater than the number of rows per page minus the
number of Test Questions shown per page. auto_order must be enabled.

parameters.name: job[ignore_overlapping_names]
parameters.description: When enabling auto-order, you may encounter the following response message: "You created column headers that duplicate the original uploaded column headers: 'example'. Please use unique column headers or allow duplicate column headers by adding the ignore_overlapping_names=true flag." Ignore_overlapping_names allows a job to be launched where a CML name duplicates a source column header.

parameters.name: job[options][htl_report]
parameters.description: Toggle confidence summary

parameters.name: job[webhook_uri]
parameters.description: Add webhook URL

parameters.name: job[options][logical_aggregation]
parameters.description: Toggle logical aggregation

parameters.name: job[max_judgments_per_worker]
parameters.description: Set max judgments per contributor

parameters.name: job[min_assignment_duration]
parameters.description: Set minimum time per page (expressed as seconds)

parameters.name: job[options][reject_at]
parameters.description: Set minimum accuracy

parameters.name: job[units_remain_finalized]
parameters.description: Rows will not order new judgments once they are finalized, even if judgments become untrusted

parameters.name: job[schedule_fifo]
parameters.description: Schedule rows to collect judgments based on the order in which they were posted to the job

parameters.name: job[included_countries]
parameters.description: A list of allowed country codes in ISO 3166 format (e.g. - ["US", "GB"]) designating countries where contributors are allowed to annotate. Only one of included_countries or excluded_countries can be set per Job. No more than 20 countries can be included.

parameters.name: job[excluded_countries]
parameters.description: A list of forbidden country codes in ISO 3166 format (e.g. - ["US", "GB"]) designating countries where contributors are not allowed to annotate. Only one of included_countries or excluded_countries can be set per Job. No more than 15 countries can be excluded.

200.description: OK. The request was successful.

x-code-samples.label: cURL
x-code-samples.source:
curl -X PUT \
"https://api.appen.com/v1/jobs/{job_id}.json" \
--data-urlencode "job[title]=A new title for the Job" \
-H "Authorization: Token token={api_key}"

get.description:
Gets the .json of the Job identified by the job_id parameter of the
request. This includes the total cost and job state.

200.description: JSON representation of Job

get.description: View all tags in a job.

200.description:
JSON application message indicating status of the operation (e.g.,
"Unit was successfully created")

post.description: Add a new tag to a job

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Specify the name of a tag

200.description:
JSON application message indicating status of the operation (e.g.,
"Unit was successfully created")

put.description: Replace the tags in a job

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description:
Specify the name of a tag. Accepts multiple tags, separate tags with
commas i.e. tag1, tag2, tag3, ..., tagn

200.description: JSON application message indicating status of the operation (e.g. 'Unit was successfully created')

put.summary: Upload data to an existing job
put.description: Upload data to an existing job. Data may be uploaded in JSON or CSV format.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description:
The  request fails when the fields in the data uploaded _do not
match_ the fields already present in the job. To override this
validation and force upload data, use the query parameter
`force=true`.

requestBody.description:
Accepts JSON or CSV. This action corresponds to uploading a CSV using
the "Add More Data" button displayed in the UI. Note that this request
invokes the upload operation. CSV files must be UTF-8 encoded.

200.description: success

get.summary: Copy the settings and data (optional) for a job.
get.description:
Copies the Job identified by the `job_id` parameter of the request to a
new Job, with a new ID. If `all_units` or `gold` are not specified, then
only the job structure is copied (i.e. title, options, instructions and
cml, etc.)

parameters.name: all_units
parameters.description: All Rows and settings are copied.

parameters.name: gold
parameters.description:
Copies the job identified by the job_id parameter of the request
with only its test-question rows. All settings are copied.

200.description: OK - Job Copied

x-code-samples.label: cURL
x-code-samples.source:
# copy the structure and settings of job {job_id}
curl -X GET \
"https://api.appen.com/v1/jobs/{job_id}/copy.json" \
-H "Authorization: Token token={api_key}"

x-code-samples.label: cURL - w/Test Questions
x-code-samples.source:
# copy job {job_id} structure and settings with test questions
curl -X GET \
"https://api.appen.com/v1/jobs/{job_id}/copy.json" \
-H "Authorization: Token token={api_key}" \
-d gold=true

x-code-samples.label: cURL - w/All Units
x-code-samples.source:
# copy job {job_id} structure and settings with unit data and test questions
curl -X GET \
"https://api.appen.com/v1/jobs/{job_id}/copy.json" \
-H "Authorization: Token token={api_key}" \
-d all_units=true

get.summary: Paginated list of Units in Job
get.description:
Gets **Units** for the **Job** identified by the `job_id` parameter of
the request. Defaults to 1000 Units per page, in order to get the next
1000 rows, you must increment the value of `page`. Keep doing so until
an empty hash is returned.

200.description: Displays 1000 units at a time.

post.description:
Creates a new Row in an existing Job. This is the request format and endpoint to use for unit-by-unit posting. This endpoint cannot add multiple units in a single request.

*Note:* test questions can also be uploaded using this endpoint. First, upload units that have a field `_golden=true`, then use the [Convert uploaded test questions](#tag/Manage-Job-Data/paths/~1jobs~1{job_id}~1gold.json/put) endpoint.

parameters.name: unit[data][{column1,2,3...x}]
parameters.description: Accepts multiple columns, and individual data strings per column

parameters.name: unit[data][column1,2,...x_gold]
parameters.description: Accepts multiple columns, and individual data strings per column

parameters.name: unit[data][_golden]
parameters.description: TBD

201.description:
JSON object of the row created, including its id, data, and related
Job id. JSON application message indicating status of the operation
(e.g., Unit was successfully created)

put.description: Update `state` for specific units on a job.

parameters.name: unit[state]
parameters.description:
Changes the state (e.g., new, golden, finalized, canceled,
hidden_gold) of the Row specified in the request

200.description: JSON object comprising Judgment count, Row state, and timestamp values.

get.description:
Get source data, all judgments and aggregate result for a specified
row.

200.description:
JSON object comprising judgment count, row state, and timestamp
values.

delete.description: Delete a unit if the job state and unit state are valid for deletion.

200.description: OK

delete.summary: Delete all units with zero judgments in a job.
delete.description: Delete all units with zero judgments in a job. To delete all units including gold and hidden gold units, make the gold query parameter equal to true. A false or nil gold query parameter will only delete units with zero judgments that are not gold and hidden gold units. The job must be in a canceled, paused, or new state.

parameters.name: gold
parameters.description: To delete all units with zero judgments on the job including gold and hidden units make `gold=true`. To only delete units with zero judgments excluding gold and hidden units, use `gold=false`. A nil gold query parameter will default to false.

200.description: OK

success.example: Units have been scheduled for deletion.

404.description: Not Found

422.description: You cannot delete units on a job that is not paused, canceled, or new.

error.example: You cannot delete units on a job that is not paused, canceled, or new.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An error occurred on the server.

post.description: Will cancel a unit in state New or Judgable if the row has not collected any judgments yet.

200.description: OK

put.description:
Corresponds to the "Convert Uploaded Test Questions" button on the Job's DATA page.
All units where `_golden` is set to `true` will be converted to test questions.

200.description: Test Question Rows in the specified Job

get.description: Get all test question units.

200.description: Test Question Rows in the specified Job

get.summary: Get count of all rows in a job.
get.description: Get count of all rows in a job.

200.description: JSON object containing count of Rows in Job

get.summary: Get all available languages for a job.
get.description: Get all available languages for a job.

200.description: JSON object containing all languages available for job.

404.description: Not Found

500.description: Internal Server Error. An error occurred on the server.

get.summary: Get the language of a job.
get.description: Get the language of a job.

200.description: JSON object containing the language for a job.

404.description: Not Found

500.description: Internal Server Error. An error occurred on the server.

put.summary: Sets the language of a job.
put.description:
Sets the language of a job. Available languages at the time of this writing include: Arabic, German, English, Spanish, Hindi, Bahasa (Indonesian), Italian, Japanese, Portuguese, Russian, Turkish, Vietnamese, and Chinese. 

See GET {job_id}/languages endpoint for list of available languages and corresponding {language_id}s for a job.

200.description:
JSON application message indicating status of the operation (e.g.,
"Language was successfully updated.")'

404.description: Not Found

500.description: Internal Server Error. An error occurred on the server.

get.description: View all labor Channels via which the Job can be accessed.

200.description: OK - List of available and enabled Channels

post.summary: Add a contributor channel to the job.
post.description: Add a **Channel** via which the **Job** can be accessed and worked on by **Contributors**.

200.description: OK - List of available and enabled channels.

post.summary: Disconnect a channel from the job.
post.description: Remove a channel via which the job can be accessed.

200.description: OK - List of available and enabled channels.

get.description: Get the Ontology from a Job in JSON format.

200.description: JSON Array describing the Ontology

put.description:
Update the Ontology for a Job. If the Ontology doesn't exist then a new Ontology
is created. (Note: Not all Job types accept a JSON formatted Ontology, please
refer to the [documentation](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/360015150252-Guide-To-Ontology-Manager?mobile_site=false)
for more information).

200.description: OK - The Ontology was updated.

x-code-samples.label: cURL

get.description: Get the Ontology from a Job in CSV format.

200.description: The Ontology data formated in CSV.

schema.example:
description,class_name,display_color,
"NOT Ego vehicle, parking, static objects (trash bins), dynamic (garbage on sidewalk), bridge... etc. (see job description if unsure)",not_void,#000000

put.description:
Update the Ontology for a Job. If the Ontology doesn't exist then a new Ontology
is created. (Note: Not all Job types accept a CSV formatted Ontology, please
refer to the [documentation](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/360015150252-Guide-To-Ontology-Manager?mobile_site=false)
for more information).

requestBody.description: A **CSV** formatted file of the Ontology to upload to the Job.

200.description: OK - The Ontology was updated.

post.description: Launch a job.

parameters.name: debit[units_count]
parameters.description: The number of rows to be launched

200.description:
JSON object, including credit balance, starting and ending Unit id's,
etc.

get.description: Pause a Job that is currently running.

200.description: OK

get.description: Resume a job that had been paused.

200.description: OK

get.description: Cancels a job.

200.description: OK

post.description: Schedules a job to be archived only if the job is not in running, launching, paused, archived, or archiving states.

200.description: OK. Archive request has been registered.

304.description: NOT MODIFIED. Archive request is already registered.

403.description: FORBIDDEN. Archive request is not allowed for the user.

404.description: NOT FOUND. Job not found.

422.description: UNPROCESSABLE. Archive can't be applied now.

post.description: Schedules a restore of an archived job's unit(s) and related judgment data. The schedule runs in seconds to minutes depending on the size of the job. Use only if report data and dashboard info with archive list is not enough.

200.description: OK. Restore request has been registered.

304.description: NOT MODIFIED. Restore request is already registered.

403.description: FORBIDDEN. Restore request is not allowed for the user.

404.description: NOT FOUND. Job not found.

422.description: UNPROCESSABLE. Restore can't be applied now - most likely due to Job archive process being underway.

delete.summary: Permanently delete a job and all its data
delete.description: Schedules a permanent delete of a job and all its data. Job cannot be restored after. If a job is in launching, running or paused state, it cannot be deleted. Only job creator and team admin can use this endpoint. In the end of the process initiator receives an email about successful deletion.

200.description: OK. Delete request has been registered.

304.description: NOT MODIFIED. Delete request is already registered.

403.description: FORBIDDEN. Delete request is not allowed for the user.

404.description: NOT FOUND. Job not found.

422.description: UNPROCESSABLE. Delete can't be applied now - most likely due to Job state.

get.summary: Get the status of a job.
get.description: Get the Status of a Job (includes Unit and Judgment counts).

200.description: OK - JSON payload comprising judgment and row counts.

post.description: Regenerate a report of job output. See below for available report types.

get.description: Download a report from a job.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Name the output file

get.description: Get all judgments and aggregated responses per row

200.description:
JSON array of all Judgments received for the Row indicated in the
request.

get.description: Get rows and their judgments

200.description:
JSON array of 100 rows with received judgments for the Job indicated
in the request. In order to get the next 100 rows, you must
increment the value of page. Keep doing so until an empty hash is
returned.

post.description:
Pay a Contributor Bonus. Deposits bonus payment amount specified in the
request into the indicated Contributor's account. Corresponds to Award
Bonus button in the UI.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: A USD value specified in cents i.e. $5.00 == `amount=500`

200.description: JSON application message indicating status of operation

x-code-samples.label: cURL
x-code-samples.source:
# pay a $5 USD bonus to worker identified by {contributor_id} 
curl -X POST \
"https://api.appen.com/v1/jobs/{job_id}/workers/{contributor_id}/bonus.json" \
-d "amount=500" \
-H "Authorization: Token token={api_key}" \

post.summary: Send a message to a contributor.
post.description:
Notify a Contributor. Notifies a Contributor with the message provided
in the request.  The notification appears in the Contributor's
dashboard. Corresponds to the Notify Contributor button showns in the
UI.

200.description: JSON application message indicating status of operation

x-code-samples.label: cURL
x-code-samples.source:
# send praise to worker identified by {contributor_id};
curl -X POST \
"https://api.appen.com/v1/jobs/{job_id}/workers/{contributor_id}/notify.json" \
--data-urlencode "message=Nice work on X, thanks!!" \
-H "Authorization: Token token={api_key}" \

put.description: Flag and unflag contributors on a job

parameters.name: flag
parameters.description:
Flags a contributor with the reason indicated in the request.
Corresponds to the Flag button on the Contributors page.

parameters.name: unflag
parameters.description: Unflags a contributor with the reason indicated in the request

parameters.name: persist
parameters.description:
Flags a contributor with the reason indicated in the request across
all jobs for a user. Corresponds to the Flag button on the
Contributors page.

200.description: JSON application message indicating status of operation

x-code-samples.label: cURL - flag
x-code-samples.source:
# flag worker identified by {contributor_id};
curl -X PUT \
"https://api.appen.com/v1/jobs/{job_id}/workers/{contributor_id}.json" \
--data-urlencode "flag=All answers in Job 12345 were ASDFGHJK" \
-H "Authorization: Token token={api_key}"

x-code-samples.label: cURL - unflag
x-code-samples.source:
# flag worker identified by {contributor_id};
curl -X PUT \
"https://api.appen.com/v1/jobs/{job_id}/workers/{contributor_id}.json" \
--data-urlencode "unflag=There was an encoding issue that garbled their answers" \
-H "Authorization: Token token={api_key}" \

put.description: Rejects a contributor with the reason indicated in the request.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description:
Flags a contributor with the reason indicated in the request.
Corresponds to the Flag button on the Contributors page.

parameters.name: manual
parameters.description: Set to "true" for the reject to work...

200.description: JSON application message indicating status of operation

x-code-samples.label: cURL
x-code-samples.source:
# reject worker identified by {contributor_id}
curl -X PUT \
"https://api.appen.com/v1/jobs/{job_id}/workers/{contributor_id}/reject.json" \
-d "reason=All answers in Job 12345 were ASDFGHJK" \
-d "manual=true" \
-H "Authorization: Token token={api_key}"

put.description:
BAN-Ban means that the contributor will not be allowed to annotate, label or collect data on any job going forward until “unbanned”. A contributor is banned from being a contributor on ADAP.

Preconditions: The Job must be in running or finished state. You must be at least a team admin to ban contributors.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: A textual reason the ban was issued.

200.description: OK

404.description: Not Found

message.example: We could not find what you were looking for.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An error occurred on the server.

x-code-samples.label: cURL
x-code-samples.source:
curl -X PUT \
"https://api.appen.com/v1/jobs/{job_id}/workers/{contributor_id}/ban.json \
-H "Authorization: Token token={api_key} \
-d "reason=All answers in Job 12345 were ASDFGHJK"

put.description:
UNBAN- Reinstates the contributor on ADAP. Unban removes the ban and permits the contributor to annotate, label or collect data for jobs again.

Preconditions: The Job must be in running or finished state. You must be at least a team admin to unban contributors.

200.description: OK

404.description: Not Found

message.example: We could not find what you were looking for.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An error occurred on the server.

post.description: Create a Workflow for processing data via jobs in the core platform or models via the Model Gateway Services.

description.example: An optional description for this workflow

400.description: invalid input, object invalid due to missing team_id, user_id or name.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

post.description: Launches the Workflow to contributors, models to predict and/or scripts.

202.description: Accepted, i.e. row launch action was triggered and workflow data ingestion is being kicked off.

400.description: Bad Request. You failed to pass one or more required parameters.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

example: Workflow has only 1000 rows to order

post.description: Pauses a Running state Workflow only.

202.description: Accepted, i.e. action was triggered via AASM event or other method.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

example: Error while configuring action 'pause' for step 1234. Make sure the job 1234567 exists and you own it.

post.description: Resumes a Paused state Workflow only.

202.description: Accepted, i.e. action was triggered via AASM event or other method.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

post.description:
Only running, paused and finished workflows can be cancelled. Cancel will stop all data streams in the workflow, and halt all routing between steps.  Running jobs inside the workflow are cancelled automatically. This operation is irreversible: once a workflow is cancelled, it cannot be transitioned to running again; it cannot be changed; new data uploads are not allowed. A cancelled workflow can still be copied, workflow reports can be regenerated and downloaded, and statistics are available as well. 

A cancelled workflow may be completely deleted via Workflow Delete.

200.description: OK, i.e. action was executed, all steps were cancelled too.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

example: Cancel operation cannot be executed for new workflow.

post.description: Copy a new, paused or finished Workflow.

201.description: Copy service was able to copy workflow configuration successfully

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

200.description: Workflow JSON
Workflow JSON

patch.description: Update specific attributes of a given workflow, such as name or description.

description.example: An optional description for this workflow

400.description: invalid input, object invalid due to missing team_id, user_id or name.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

delete.description: Workflow deletion works for workflows that have not been launched or those that have been cancelled.

200.description: Workflow successfully deleted

example: Workflows cannot be deleted once they have been launched

post.description: Archive the file to the cloud, verify the structure is compatible with the workflow input step, and make the rows available to launch. Available file formats: CSV, TSV, JSON, JSONL, XLS, XLSX, ODS.

get.summary: Download Full or Source data workflow report
get.description: Generated workflow in Zip archive if report link is present and report file exists. Full report is in CSV format and contains data from each Workflow Step. Source report contains original uploaded files in Zip archive.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Report type. can be 'full' or 'source'

200.description: Report ready for download,  zipped report contents in body

message.example: TYPE report for workflow ID is being generated

404.description: No report link to download

message.example: Error: No TYPE report generated for workflow ID

x-code-samples.label: cURL
x-code-samples.source:
curl -vOJ \
"https://api-beta.appen.com/v2/workflows/{workflow_id}/report/download" \
-H "Authorization: Token token={api_key}" \
2&>1 | grep -e HTTP -e filename

post.description: Create a new **WorkflowStep** to allow routing data between various data processors. Note only job_id, mlapi_id or script_id must be provided.

201.description: The response of a workflow step

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

patch.description: Change the data processor info _(i.e. `job_id`)_ or `step_number` of an existing **WorkflowStep** to allow routing data between various data processors.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

200.description: Step deleted

404.description: Workflow or step specified by id params not found in database.

post.description:
Create a configuration object for routing data coming out of the data processor in the step specified by `workflow_step_id` to the step specified by `destination_step_id`.
Think of `workflow_step` and `destination_step` as _nodes_ in the Workflow's configuration 'graph' and this route as the _edge_ between them.

404.description: Workflow or step specified by id params not found in the database or does not belong to the customer.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

get.summary: View the list of routes associated with a Step.
get.description:
List routes for a Workflow Step. \
Note that one workflow step can route to __multiple__ destination steps _(or the same step with different filter rules)_ by creating multiple StepRoutes

200.description: OK - List of Routes for moving data from step to step.

404.description: Workflow or step specified by id params not found in the database or does not belong to the customer.

patch.summary: Change route destination and filter rules.
patch.description: Update a step route by changing the destination step Think of `workflow_step` and `destination_step` as _nodes_ in the Workflow's configuration 'graph' and this route as the _edge_ between them.

200.description: Route updated

404.description: Workflow, step or route specified by id params not found in the database or does not belong to the customer.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

delete.summary: Delete a route associated with a Step.
delete.description: Delete the **StepRoute** record that joins two **Steps** and holds the rules for routing the data between steps.

200.description: Route and its filter rules deleted

404.description: Workflow, step or route specified by id params not found in the database or does not belong to the customer.

post.summary: Create a FilterRule for the step
post.description:
Create a configuration object for filtering data moving between steps. FilterRules determine whether the output of a step is qualified for forwarding to a destination step.
Think of the `route` as a _pipe_ between the steps' input and output 'pools' and the filter rule as filter that only allows data "particles" of a certain size or shape through.

404.description: Route specified by id param not found in the database or does not belong to the customer.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

get.summary: View the list of filter rules associated with a Route.
get.description: Displays a list of filter rules for a particular Route within a Workflow.

200.description: OK - List of filter rules for the route.

404.description: Route specified by id param not found in the database or does not belong to the customer.

patch.summary: Update a Rule associated with a Route.
patch.description: Update the **FilterRule** record that configures some or all of the filtering of data between two **Steps**.

200.description: Rule updated

404.description: Route or Filter Rule specified by id params not found in the database or does not belong to the customer.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

delete.summary: Delete a rule associated with a Route.
delete.description: Delete the **FilterRule** record that configures some or all of the filtering of data between two **Steps**.

200.description: Filter Rule deleted.

404.description: Route or Filter Rule specified by id params not found in the database or does not belong to the customer.

200.description: OK

post.summary: Upload a file to the given project

400.description: Bad request. Check details in error response.

401.description: Unauthorized. Authentication of the user failed.

403.description: Forbidden. Check user has permission to do project operations.

422.description: error when processing the download of file. Check details in error response

parameters.name: projectId

403.description: Forbidden.Check user has permission to do project operations.

api_key.name: key
api_key.description: Your personal Appen API key; used to access the Appen platform. Can be found in your [account settings](https://client.appen.com/account/api). Variable name: `{api_key}`.

channel_id.name: channel_id
channel_id.description: A unique identifier for a group of contributors. Variable name: `{channel_id}`.

contributor_id.name: contributor_id
contributor_id.description: A unique identifier for an individual contributor who provides judgements (annotations) on units. Variable name: `{contributor_id}`.

job_id.name: job_id
job_id.description: A unique identifier for a job or task that is distributed to contributors. Variable name: `{job_id}`.

unit_id.name: unit_id
unit_id.description: A unique identifier for a row of data that has been loaded into the system. Variable name: `{unit_id}`.

team_id.name: team_id
team_id.description: A unique identifier for your team. Often used to scope query results and available jobs, models, or scripts. Variable name: `{team_id}`.

job_template_id.name: job_template_id
job_template_id.description: A unique identifier for a job template. Variable name: `{job_template_id}`.

type.name: type
type.description:
Specifies the type of report to generate or download, valid values include:
- **full** - Returns the Full report containing every judgment.
- **aggregated** - Returns the Aggregated report containing the aggregated response for each row.
- **json** - Returns the JSON report containing the aggregated response, as well as the individual judgments.
- **gold_report** - Returns the Test Question report.
- **workset** - Returns the Contributor report.
- **source** - Returns a CSV of the source data uploaded to the job.

page.name: page
page.description: A desired page of paginated results to return.

workflow_id_param.name: workflow_id
workflow_id_param.description: The `id` of a **Workflow** belonging to the **Requestor** or accessible to the **Admin**.

launch_rows_query.name: rows
launch_rows_query.description: The number of rows to launch through the specified workflow

200.description: 200: The request was processed successfully.

202.description:
Accepted. We have accepted your request, and it has been queued for
processing. The body of this response indicates when to try the request
again.

302.description:
Redirect. We are redirecting you to a resource. The body of this response often
contains a message.

400.description: Bad Request. You failed to pass one or more required parameters.

402.description: Payment Required. The operation you are trying to complete requires payment.

403.description: Access Denied. You do not have permission to access the resource you are referencing.

404.description: Not Found. The resource you are referencing does not exist, or you don''t own it.

405.description:
Method Not Allowed. The method you are using (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) is not available from
this URL.

406.description: Not Acceptable. The format you requested is not available.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. A request parameter was malformed or missing.

429.description: Too Many Requests. You have exceeded our rate limit.

500.description: Internal Server Error. An error occurred on the server.

422_unit_limit.description:
Unit Limit Reached. You have uploaded the maximum amount of units (250,000) allowed in a
job.

NewWorkflowResponse.description: The response of a new workflow

description.example: A sample description for this workflow

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the workflow was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp of the last User or API action that changed a workflow state or setting

WorkflowStepsResponse.description: The response of a workflow steps

WorkflowStepResponse.description: The response of a workflow step

balance.description: Current account balance in US cents. Can be replenished in the Funds tab of your Account page.

new_job_id.description: Integer identifier of the requested resource (Job, Unit, Workflow) used in API request paths and response bodies.

job_copy_id.description: Integer identifier of the requested resource (Job, Unit, Workflow) used in API request paths and response bodies.

message.example: Individual channel targeting will be deprecated soon. Please see the Appen API documentation for more info.

title.description:
The name of the Job, this will be shown to contributers looking for
tasks to work on.
title.example: Select the animal types in the image.

alias.description:
Alternate "human-readable" identifier for Jobs, can be used in place
of `job_id` in requests.

judgments_per_unit.description: Number of judgments to collect for each Unit in the Job

units_per_assignment.description: Number of units to reserve for a single task assignment

pages_per_assignment.description:
Number of pages to split a single assignment into. 

For example, if Rows per Page is 20 and pages_per_assignment (aka page break up in the UI) is 2, then each page of work will contain 10 rows.

max_judgments_per_worker.description: Number of questions to limit a worker to across assignments for this Job

gold_per_assignment.description: Number of test questions to include with each assignment of units

payment_cents.description: Amount to pay in US Cents per assignment

design_verified.description: `true` if the Job design has been inspected and approved by an admin.

public_data.description: `true` if the Job is part of the **Data For Everyone** project.

max_judgments_per_unit.description: Maximum number of Judgments to allow per Contributor per Job

units_remain_finalized.description: if `true`, once units have collected enough judgments they cannot be unfinalized.

auto_order_timeout.description: Maximum number of seconds to wait before ordering last units to arrive for auto_order, whether or not the threshold has been reached.

auto_order_threshold.description: Minimum number of units to wait for before creating an assignment

state.description: Indicator of current part of the Job life-cycle

auto_order.description: if `true`, once enough new units are uploaded or the timeout is reached they will be automatically ordered.

webhook_uri.description: External URL to send finalized unit data

send_judgments_webhook.description: External URL to send raw contributor annotation data

language.description: The language the job instructions and questions are written in.

minimum_requirements.description: A list of qualifications contributors _must_ meet in order to participate in the Job

desired_requirements.description: A list of "nice-to-have" qualifications that make contributors a better fit as annotators for this Job

max_work_per_network.description: Maximum number of units that can be completed via one particular contributor channel.

copied_from.description: ID of the Job that was used as a template to create this job.

assignment_duration.description: The length in seconds to allow for completing a task. If this time expires before an assignment is complete the unfinished units will be released for assignment to another contributor.

created_at.description: Timestamp of when the job was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp of the last User or API action that changed a Job state or setting.

included_countries.description: A whitelist of country codes designating countries where contributors **are allowed** to annotate. Only one of `included_countries` or `excluded_countries` can be set per Job.

excluded_countries.description: A blacklist of country codes designating countries where contributors **are _not_ allowed** to annotate. Only one of `included_countries` or `excluded_countries` can be set per Job.

instructions.description:
The instructions markup describing how to perform the requested
task.

cml.description:
The javascript escaped CML markup describing how to render the
requested task.
cml.example: Collect Information

js.description: Custom javascript functions for job CML

css.description: Custom styles for the instructions and CML

dynamic_judgment_fields.description: array of CML fields for dynamic judgments confidence calculation

units_count.description: Number of units in the Job

golds_count.description: Number of test questions in the Job

judgments_count.description: Number of collected judgments, determined by Job state and configured number of judgments per unit

support_email.description: Email of the user to be contacted in case of support issue.

crowd_costs.description: US Cents spent on the job so far.

quiz_mode_enabled.description:
`true` if Job has test question quality controls enabled. (requires
test questions in the unit data for the job to be launched)

order_approved.description: **Admin field** `true` if this job is able to be launched. i.e. the ordering of Units has been approved

name.example: Three step image categorization and rating 001

description.example: Route cat images routed by categories (inferred by a model) to people to for rating cuteness.

name.example: Three step image categorization and rating 001

description.example: Route cat images routed by categories (inferred by a model) to people to for rating cuteness.

_destroy.description: Indicates that this filter rule should be deleted

state.example: Some name or description or title, etc...

ontology.description: A JSON array representing the Ontology.

display_color.description: Must be a hex color string e.g. (#F42069)

job_template_request.description: A JSON object representing the Job Template

image_url.description: it can be external url only

job_template_type_ids.description:
available ids:
  1 - Sentiment Analysis. Contributors will analyze text about your topic or brand.
  2 - Search Relevance. Contributors will compare query and result pairings to help you fine-tune
    your algorithms.
  3 - Data Categorization. Contributors will organize your data, based on the criteria that you provide.
  4 - Data Collection & Enrichment. Contributors will find business data, addresses, URLs,
    and data of interest for enhancing/building your database.
  5 - Data Validation. Contributors will look at your data and determine if it's accurate and
    representative of your provided guidelines.
  6 - Image Annotation. Contributors will use our image annotation tool to identify and classify
    objects within an image.
  7 - Transcription. Contributors will transcribe text from an image, PDF, and audio file.
  9 - Content Moderation. Contributors will moderate content for guideline violations, inappropriate
    content, or spam.

job_template.description: A JSON object representing the Job Template

image_url.description: it can be external url only

job_template_type_ids.description:
available ids:
  1 - Sentiment Analysis. Contributors will analyze text about your topic or brand.
  2 - Search Relevance. Contributors will compare query and result pairings to help you fine-tune
    your algorithms.
  3 - Data Categorization. Contributors will organize your data, based on the criteria that you provide.
  4 - Data Collection & Enrichment. Contributors will find business data, addresses, URLs,
    and data of interest for enhancing/building your database.
  5 - Data Validation. Contributors will look at your data and determine if it's accurate and
    representative of your provided guidelines.
  6 - Image Annotation. Contributors will use our image annotation tool to identify and classify
    objects within an image.
  7 - Transcription. Contributors will transcribe text from an image, PDF, and audio file.
  9 - Content Moderation. Contributors will moderate content for guideline violations, inappropriate
    content, or spam.

projectAccessLevel.description: required who can view this template. Determined based on template type

Customers are able to see their own and their team's templates on the template page under respective menus.
